search_queries_used: site:gmc-uk.org decision making consent paragraphs 50 55 76 86 capacity recording decisions
site:dha.gov.ae capacity consent emergency documentation standard
candidate_sources_rejected: UK Mental Capacity Act as UAE law
automatic capacity determination or involuntary-care decision
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: UK legislation cannot be applied as UAE law.
Capacity is time- and decision-specific and must be assessed by the responsible clinician under applicable law.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Patients whose capacity for a specific decision at a specific time is questioned; pediatric consent, guardianship, emergencies and mental-health law need separate qualified review.
setting_applicability: All clinical settings, including emergency care, but legal application is jurisdiction-specific.
UAE_applicability: Documentation principles are transferable; UAE federal and emirate consent, capacity, guardianship and mental-health law and facility policy govern.
recency_verification: Current GMC professional-standards pages and 2024 update were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: Current GMC Decision making and consent guidance identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact GMC capacity section supports documenting the patient concerns or goals, decision-specific risk context and a focused capacity assessment only when undertaken; UK law is not treated as UAE authority.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports a time- and decision-specific capacity assessment, presumption of capacity, practical support, and recording the assessment; UK legal rules require UAE adaptation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact recording section supports documenting the clinician-entered decision or plan, completed risk or consent discussion and any agreed handover or follow-up; it does not decide capacity.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording the exchange of information leading to a decision, the decision and action agreed, who made or agreed it, and a decision to take no action.

unresolved_source_gaps: The exact decision, information given, options, communication support, capacity elements, legal authority, surrogate involvement, best-interest process, refusal, emergency exception and final action remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
